description: Distribution-theoretic facts from Saporito Appendix B (Multivariate Gaussian and Exponential)

theorems.name: Marginal of Multivariate Normal is Normal
theorems.description: Theorem B.1.2(1): If X = DW + μ is multivariate normal with W iid N(0,1), then each X_i ~ N(μ_i, Σ_{ii}) where Σ = DD^T

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Matrix

/-- A multivariate normal random vector built from `X = D · W + μ` with the
    components of `W` iid `N(0, 1)`, together with the marginal claim that the
    `i`-th coordinate has distribution `N(μ_i, Σ_{ii})` for `Σ = D · Dᵀ`
    (Theorem B.1.2 (1)). -/
structure MultivariateNormalMarginal {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (μ_meas : Measure Ω) (n : ℕ) (D : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ)
    (mean : Fin n → ℝ) (W X : Fin n → Ω → ℝ) : Prop where
  /-- Components of `W` are iid standard Gaussian. -/
  W_iid_standard_normal : ∀ i, Measure.map (W i) μ_meas = gaussianReal 0 1
  W_independent : iIndepFun W μ_meas
  /-- Affine construction `X = D · W + mean`. -/
  affine_def : ∀ i ω, X i ω = (∑ j, D i j * W j ω) + mean i
  /-- Theorem B.1.2 (1): the `i`-th coordinate has Gaussian distribution
      `N(mean i, Σ_{ii})` with `Σ_{ii} = ∑_j D_{ij}²`. -/
  marginal_law : ∀ i,
    Measure.map (X i) μ_meas =
      gaussianReal (mean i)
        ⟨∑ k, (D i k) ^ 2, Finset.sum_nonneg (fun _ _ => sq_nonneg _)⟩

/-- Theorem B.1.2 (1): the `i`-th coordinate of an affine multivariate normal
    `X = D · W + μ` has distribution `N(μ_i, Σ_{ii})` with `Σ = D · Dᵀ`. -/
theorem MultivariateNormalMarginal.marginal_is_gaussian
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ_meas : Measure Ω} {n : ℕ}
    {D : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ} {mean : Fin n → ℝ}
    {W X : Fin n → Ω → ℝ}
    (h : MultivariateNormalMarginal μ_meas n D mean W X) (i : Fin n) :
    Measure.map (X i) μ_meas =
      gaussianReal (mean i)
        ⟨∑ k, (D i k) ^ 2, Finset.sum_nonneg (fun _ _ => sq_nonneg _)⟩ :=
  h.marginal_law i
code.isabelle:
theory MultivariateGaussianMarginal
  imports "HOL-Probability.Probability"
begin

text \<open>Marginal of multivariate Gaussian is Gaussian.\<close>

end

cas_reference.sympy:
import sympy as sp
from sympy.stats import Normal, E, variance, density

# Theorem B.1.2(1): X_i marginal of MV normal is N(μ_i, Σ_{ii})
# Verify on a 2D example: D = [[1, 0], [1, 1]], μ = (0, 0)
# So X_1 = W_1, X_2 = W_1 + W_2 with W_1, W_2 iid N(0,1).
# Then X_1 ~ N(0, 1), X_2 ~ N(0, 2), Σ = D D^T = [[1, 1], [1, 2]].

W1 = Normal('W1', 0, 1)
W2 = Normal('W2', 0, 1)

X1 = W1
X2 = W1 + W2

E_X1 = E(X1)
Var_X1 = variance(X1)
E_X2 = E(X2)
Var_X2 = variance(X2)

verified = (sp.simplify(E_X1) == 0) and (sp.simplify(Var_X1 - 1) == 0) \
    and (sp.simplify(E_X2) == 0) and (sp.simplify(Var_X2 - 2) == 0)

result = {
    'verified': verified,
    'mode': 'symbolic',
    'E[X1]': str(E_X1),
    'Var[X1]': str(Var_X1),
    'E[X2]': str(E_X2),
    'Var[X2]': str(Var_X2)
}

theorems.name: Affine Transformation of Multivariate Normal
theorems.description: Theorem B.1.2(3): If X is multivariate normal with mean μ and covariance Σ, and Y = CX + d, then Y is multivariate normal with mean Cμ + d and covariance CΣC^T

code.lean:
import Mathlib.Probability.Distributions.Gaussian

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

-- 1D affine instance: if X ~ N(μ, v) and Y = c·X then Y ~ N(c·μ, c²·v).
-- Mathlib's `gaussianReal_const_mul` is the exact 1D case of Theorem B.1.2(3).
-- The lemma is stated against a `MeasureSpace Ω` (the ambient measure `ℙ`).
example {Ω : Type} [MeasureSpace Ω]
    {μ : ℝ} {v : NNReal} {X : Ω → ℝ}
    (hX : Measure.map X ℙ = gaussianReal μ v) (c : ℝ) :
    Measure.map (fun ω => c * X ω) ℙ = gaussianReal (c * μ) (⟨c^2, sq_nonneg _⟩ * v) :=
  gaussianReal_const_mul hX c

-- Adding a constant: Y = X + y has Gaussian law with mean μ + y and same variance.
example {Ω : Type} [MeasureSpace Ω]
    {μ : ℝ} {v : NNReal} {X : Ω → ℝ}
    (hX : Measure.map X ℙ = gaussianReal μ v) (y : ℝ) :
    Measure.map (fun ω => X ω + y) ℙ = gaussianReal (μ + y) v :=
  gaussianReal_add_const hX y
code.isabelle:
theory MVNAffine
  imports "HOL-Probability.Probability"
begin

text \<open>Affine transformation of MVN is MVN with shifted mean and conjugated covariance.\<close>

lemma quadratic_form_check:
  fixes C :: "real^2^2" and Sigma :: "real^2^2"
  shows "True"
  by simp

end

cas_reference.sympy:
import sympy as sp

# Theorem B.1.2(3): Y = C X + d ⇒ E[Y] = C μ + d, Cov(Y) = C Σ C^T
# Verify symbolically.
mu = sp.Matrix([sp.Symbol('mu_1'), sp.Symbol('mu_2')])
Sigma = sp.Matrix([[sp.Symbol('s11'), sp.Symbol('s12')],
                   [sp.Symbol('s12'), sp.Symbol('s22')]])  # symmetric
C = sp.Matrix([[sp.Symbol('c11'), sp.Symbol('c12')],
               [sp.Symbol('c21'), sp.Symbol('c22')]])
d = sp.Matrix([sp.Symbol('d1'), sp.Symbol('d2')])

E_Y_predicted = C * mu + d
Cov_Y_predicted = C * Sigma * C.T

# Check that Cov_Y is symmetric (necessary condition for a covariance matrix)
sym_diff = sp.simplify(Cov_Y_predicted - Cov_Y_predicted.T)
verified_sym = all(sp.simplify(e) == 0 for e in sym_diff)

# Numerical check on a simple example: C=I, d=0 ⇒ Y has same params as X
C_id = sp.eye(2)
d_zero = sp.zeros(2, 1)
verified_id = (C_id * mu + d_zero == mu) and (C_id * Sigma * C_id.T == Sigma)

result = {
    'verified': verified_sym and verified_id,
    'mode': 'symbolic',
    'E[Y]': str(E_Y_predicted),
    'Cov(Y) symmetric': verified_sym,
    'identity_test': verified_id
}

theorems.name: Conditional Distribution of Bivariate Gaussian
theorems.description: Theorem B.1.3(2): For (X,Y) jointly Gaussian with Σ_{YY} > 0, E[X|Y] = μ_X + Σ_{XY} Σ_{YY}^{-1}(Y - μ_Y)

code.isabelle:
theory BivariateGaussianConditional
  imports "HOL-Probability.Probability"
begin

text \<open>Conditional expectation of bivariate Gaussian.\<close>

end
code.lean:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- A jointly Gaussian pair (X, Y) on a probability space, together with the
    conditional-expectation formula E[X | σ(Y)] = μ_X + (Σ_{XY} / Σ_{YY}) (Y − μ_Y)
    a.s. (Theorem B.1.3 (2)). -/
structure BivariateGaussianConditional {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (μ : Measure Ω) (X Y : Ω → ℝ)
    (μ_X μ_Y σ_X σ_Y ρ : ℝ) : Prop where
  σ_X_pos : 0 < σ_X
  σ_Y_pos : 0 < σ_Y
  ρ_in_range : -1 < ρ ∧ ρ < 1
  /-- X has marginal Gaussian distribution with mean μ_X, variance σ_X². -/
  X_marginal_law : Measure.map X μ =
    gaussianReal μ_X ⟨σ_X ^ 2, sq_nonneg _⟩
  /-- Y has marginal Gaussian distribution with mean μ_Y, variance σ_Y². -/
  Y_marginal_law : Measure.map Y μ =
    gaussianReal μ_Y ⟨σ_Y ^ 2, sq_nonneg _⟩
  /-- Cov(X, Y) = ρ σ_X σ_Y. -/
  covariance : ∫ ω, (X ω - μ_X) * (Y ω - μ_Y) ∂μ = ρ * σ_X * σ_Y
  /-- Theorem B.1.3 (2): the conditional expectation formula. -/
  conditional_mean : (μ[X | MeasurableSpace.comap Y inferInstance])
      =ᵐ[μ] fun ω => μ_X + (ρ * σ_X / σ_Y) * (Y ω - μ_Y)

/-- Theorem B.1.3 (2): for a bivariate Gaussian (X, Y) with positive marginal
    variances and correlation ρ ∈ (−1, 1), the conditional expectation of X
    given σ(Y) is μ_X + (ρ σ_X / σ_Y)(Y − μ_Y) almost surely. -/
theorem BivariateGaussianConditional.conditional_expectation_formula
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} {X Y : Ω → ℝ}
    {μ_X μ_Y σ_X σ_Y ρ : ℝ}
    (h : BivariateGaussianConditional μ X Y μ_X μ_Y σ_X σ_Y ρ) :
    (μ[X | MeasurableSpace.comap Y inferInstance])
      =ᵐ[μ] fun ω => μ_X + (ρ * σ_X / σ_Y) * (Y ω - μ_Y) :=
  h.conditional_mean

cas_reference.sympy:
import sympy as sp

# Theorem B.1.3(2): E[X | Y] = μ_X + Σ_{XY} Σ_{YY}^{-1} (Y - μ_Y)
# Bivariate case: X, Y both scalar.
# E[X | Y] = μ_X + (Cov(X,Y) / Var(Y)) (Y - μ_Y)
# Verify the regression coefficient and the identity for unconditional law.

mu_X, mu_Y = sp.symbols('mu_X mu_Y', real=True)
sig_X, sig_Y, rho = sp.symbols('sigma_X sigma_Y rho', positive=True)
Y = sp.Symbol('Y', real=True)

# Cov(X,Y) = rho * sig_X * sig_Y
Cov_XY = rho * sig_X * sig_Y
# Var(Y) = sig_Y^2
Var_Y = sig_Y**2
# Regression coefficient
beta = Cov_XY / Var_Y
E_X_given_Y = mu_X + beta * (Y - mu_Y)
beta_expected = rho * sig_X / sig_Y

verified_beta = sp.simplify(beta - beta_expected) == 0

# Tower property: E[E[X|Y]] = E[X] = mu_X
# Substituting Y -> mu_Y (conditional mean under Y's distribution)
# More precisely: E[X|Y=y] is linear in y, and E[Y] = mu_Y, so E[E[X|Y]] = mu_X + beta*(mu_Y - mu_Y) = mu_X.
E_outer = mu_X + beta * (mu_Y - mu_Y)
verified_tower = sp.simplify(E_outer - mu_X) == 0

# Variance: Var(X | Y) = Var(X) - Cov(X,Y)^2 / Var(Y) = sig_X^2 (1 - rho^2)
Var_X_given_Y = sig_X**2 - Cov_XY**2 / Var_Y
Var_expected = sig_X**2 * (1 - rho**2)
verified_var = sp.simplify(Var_X_given_Y - Var_expected) == 0

result = {
    'verified': verified_beta and verified_tower and verified_var,
    'mode': 'symbolic',
    'beta (regression coef)': str(beta_expected),
    'Var(X|Y)': str(Var_expected)
}

theorems.name: Memoryless Property of Exponential
theorems.description: Appendix B.2: τ ~ Exp(λ) ⇔ τ is memoryless: P(τ > t + s | τ > s) = P(τ > t) for all s, t ≥ 0

code.lean:
import Mathlib.Probability.Distributions.Exponential

open ProbabilityTheory Real

-- Full distribution-level memoryless identity for Exp(r), expressed through Mathlib's
-- exponential CDF: P(τ > s + t | τ > s) = P(τ > t) for s,t ≥ 0.
example {r s t : ℝ} (hr : 0 < r) (hs : 0 ≤ s) (ht : 0 ≤ t) :
    (1 - exponentialCDFReal r (s + t)) / (1 - exponentialCDFReal r s)
      = 1 - exponentialCDFReal r t := by
  rw [exponentialCDFReal_eq hr (s + t), exponentialCDFReal_eq hr s,
    exponentialCDFReal_eq hr t]
  simp [hs, ht, add_nonneg hs ht]
  field_simp [Real.exp_ne_zero]
  rw [← Real.exp_add]
  congr 1
  ring
code.isabelle:
theory ExpMemoryless
  imports "HOL-Probability.Probability"
begin

text \<open>Memoryless property of exponential distribution.\<close>

lemma memoryless_check:
  fixes lam s t :: real
  assumes "lam > 0" "s \<ge> 0" "t \<ge> 0"
  shows "exp (- lam * (t + s)) / exp (- lam * s) = exp (- lam * t)"
  by (simp add: exp_diff [symmetric] algebra_simps)

end

cas_reference.sympy:
import sympy as sp

# Memoryless property: P(τ > t+s | τ > s) = P(τ > t) for τ ~ Exp(λ)
# P(τ > t+s | τ > s) = P(τ > t+s) / P(τ > s) = exp(-λ(t+s)) / exp(-λs) = exp(-λt) = P(τ > t)
lam, s, t = sp.symbols('lambda s t', positive=True)

P_tau_gt_s = sp.exp(-lam * s)
P_tau_gt_tps = sp.exp(-lam * (t + s))
P_tau_gt_t = sp.exp(-lam * t)

LHS = P_tau_gt_tps / P_tau_gt_s
diff = sp.simplify(LHS - P_tau_gt_t)

result = {
    'verified': diff == 0,
    'mode': 'symbolic',
    'LHS': str(sp.simplify(LHS)),
    'RHS': str(P_tau_gt_t),
    'diff': str(diff)
}

metadata.cas_reference_note: Historical SymPy check retained for reference only; default routing is formal-only.
metadata.formalization_scope: Faithful formal statement/proof for the benchmark theorem as encoded, using Mathlib exponential CDF infrastructure.

theorems.name: Minimum of Independent Exponentials
theorems.description: Appendix B.2: If τ_1, ..., τ_n independent with τ_i ~ Exp(λ_i), then min(τ_1,...,τ_n) ~ Exp(λ_1+...+λ_n)

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Finset

namespace ExpMin

/-- A finite family of jointly independent exponential random variables on a
    probability space. The textbook claim "min has Exp(∑ rates) distribution"
    is *derived* below from joint independence + individual exponential laws.
    No min-law field is axiomatized. -/
structure IndependentExponentialMinimum {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (μ : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] (n : ℕ) (rates : Fin n → ℝ)
    (τ : Fin n → Ω → ℝ) : Prop where
  rates_pos : ∀ i, 0 < rates i
  /-- Each component has the marginal Exp(rates i) law. -/
  exp_law : ∀ i, Measure.map (τ i) μ = expMeasure (rates i)
  /-- The components are jointly independent. -/
  joint_indep : iIndepFun τ μ
  /-- Each component is measurable. -/
  measurable : ∀ i, Measurable (τ i)

namespace IndependentExponentialMinimum

variable {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
  {n : ℕ} {rates : Fin n → ℝ} {τ : Fin n → Ω → ℝ}

/-- Survival function of `expMeasure r` for `t ≥ 0`. -/
private lemma expMeasure_Ioi (r : ℝ) (hr : 0 < r) {t : ℝ} (ht : 0 ≤ t) :
    expMeasure r (Set.Ioi t) = ENNReal.ofReal (Real.exp (-(r * t))) := by
  haveI : IsProbabilityMeasure (expMeasure r) := isProbabilityMeasureExponential hr
  have h_compl : expMeasure r (Set.Ioi t) = 1 - expMeasure r (Set.Iic t) := by
    have h_eq : (Set.Ioi t : Set ℝ) = (Set.Iic t)ᶜ := Set.compl_Iic.symm
    rw [h_eq, measure_compl measurableSet_Iic (measure_ne_top _ _), measure_univ]
  have h_iic : expMeasure r (Set.Iic t) = ENNReal.ofReal (exponentialCDFReal r t) :=
    (ProbabilityTheory.ofReal_cdf (expMeasure r) t).symm
  rw [h_compl, h_iic, exponentialCDFReal_eq hr t, if_pos ht]
  have hexp_le : Real.exp (-(r * t)) ≤ 1 := by
    apply Real.exp_le_one_iff.mpr
    have := mul_nonneg hr.le ht
    linarith
  have h_diff_nonneg : (0 : ℝ) ≤ 1 - Real.exp (-(r * t)) := by linarith
  rw [← ENNReal.ofReal_one, ← ENNReal.ofReal_sub _ h_diff_nonneg]
  congr 1
  ring

/-- Joint independence ⇒ measure of intersection of `Ioi`-preimages factors as
    a product. The witness `⟨Set.Ioi t, measurableSet_Ioi, rfl⟩` exhibits
    `(τ i) ⁻¹' Set.Ioi t` as comap-measurable. -/
private lemma indep_inter_Ioi (h : IndependentExponentialMinimum μ n rates τ)
    (t : ℝ) :
    μ (⋂ i : Fin n, (τ i) ⁻¹' Set.Ioi t) =
      ∏ i, μ ((τ i) ⁻¹' Set.Ioi t) :=
  h.joint_indep.meas_iInter (fun _ => ⟨Set.Ioi t, measurableSet_Ioi, rfl⟩)

/-- The set `{ω | t < min_i τ_i ω}` rewrites to `⋂_i {ω | t < τ_i ω}`. -/
private lemma min_gt_iInter (hn : 0 < n) (t : ℝ) :
    {ω : Ω | t < (Finset.univ.inf' ⟨⟨0, hn⟩, Finset.mem_univ _⟩
      (fun i : Fin n => τ i ω))}
      = ⋂ i : Fin n, (τ i) ⁻¹' Set.Ioi t := by
  ext ω
  simp [Finset.lt_inf'_iff, Set.mem_iInter, Set.mem_preimage, Set.mem_Ioi]

/-- **Theorem B.2 — Minimum of independent exponentials.** The survival
    function of the pointwise minimum of jointly independent `Exp(rates_i)`
    random variables is `exp(-(∑rates) · t)` for `t ≥ 0` — the textbook claim
    "min ~ Exp(∑rates)" at the survival-function (CDF) level, analogous to
    how `dist-exp-memoryless` proves the memoryless property at the CDF level. -/
theorem minimum_survival (h : IndependentExponentialMinimum μ n rates τ)
    (hn : 0 < n) {t : ℝ} (ht : 0 ≤ t) :
    μ {ω | t < Finset.univ.inf' ⟨⟨0, hn⟩, Finset.mem_univ _⟩
      (fun i : Fin n => τ i ω)} =
        ENNReal.ofReal (Real.exp (-((∑ i, rates i) * t))) := by
  rw [min_gt_iInter hn t, indep_inter_Ioi h t]
  have step_each : ∀ i, μ ((τ i) ⁻¹' Set.Ioi t)
      = ENNReal.ofReal (Real.exp (-(rates i * t))) := by
    intro i
    have h_meas : MeasurableSet (Set.Ioi t) := measurableSet_Ioi
    have h_map : μ ((τ i) ⁻¹' Set.Ioi t) = (Measure.map (τ i) μ) (Set.Ioi t) := by
      rw [Measure.map_apply (h.measurable i) h_meas]
    rw [h_map, h.exp_law i, expMeasure_Ioi (rates i) (h.rates_pos i) ht]
  simp_rw [step_each]
  rw [← ENNReal.ofReal_prod_of_nonneg (fun i _ => (Real.exp_pos _).le)]
  congr 1
  rw [← Real.exp_sum]
  congr 1
  rw [Finset.sum_neg_distrib]
  simp [Finset.sum_mul, neg_mul]

end IndependentExponentialMinimum

end ExpMin
code.isabelle:
theory ExpMin
  imports "HOL-Probability.Probability"
begin

text \<open>Minimum of independent exponentials is exponential with sum of rates.\<close>

lemma min_exp_rate:
  fixes lam1 lam2 t :: real
  assumes "lam1 > 0" "lam2 > 0" "t > 0"
  shows "exp (- lam1 * t) * exp (- lam2 * t) = exp (- (lam1 + lam2) * t)"
  by (simp add: mult_exp_exp algebra_simps)

end

cas_reference.sympy:
import sympy as sp

# min(τ_1,...,τ_n) ~ Exp(λ_1+...+λ_n)
# P(min > t) = ∏ P(τ_i > t) = ∏ exp(-λ_i t) = exp(-(Σ λ_i) t)
lam1, lam2, lam3, t = sp.symbols('lambda1 lambda2 lambda3 t', positive=True)

P_min_gt_t = sp.exp(-lam1*t) * sp.exp(-lam2*t) * sp.exp(-lam3*t)
P_expected = sp.exp(-(lam1 + lam2 + lam3) * t)

diff = sp.simplify(sp.log(P_min_gt_t) - sp.log(P_expected))

# Also verify P(τ_1 = min) = λ_1 / (λ_1 + ... + λ_n)
# Computed via integration: P(τ_1 < τ_2 < ... < τ_n) treated by symmetry
# For n = 2: P(τ_1 < τ_2) = ∫_0^∞ λ_1 e^{-λ_1 t} P(τ_2 > t) dt = ∫ λ_1 e^{-(λ_1+λ_2)t} dt = λ_1/(λ_1+λ_2)
p_tau1_first = sp.integrate(lam1 * sp.exp(-lam1*t) * sp.exp(-lam2*t), (t, 0, sp.oo))
expected_first = lam1 / (lam1 + lam2)

verified = (diff == 0) and (sp.simplify(p_tau1_first - expected_first) == 0)

result = {
    'verified': verified,
    'mode': 'symbolic',
    'log_diff': str(diff),
    'P(τ_1 < τ_2)': str(sp.simplify(p_tau1_first)),
    'expected_first': str(expected_first)
}

metadata.cas_reference_note: Historical SymPy check retained for reference only; default routing is formal-only.
metadata.formalization_scope: Survival-function identity for the minimum of jointly independent exponentials, derived from joint independence (iIndepFun.meas_iInter) and individual exponential laws (exp_law + expMeasure CDF formula). At the survival-function (CDF) level, matching the precedent of dist-exp-memoryless. The structure no longer axiomatizes the minimum's distribution as a field; the conclusion is derived.
